userStories.title: Define CLI contract and output layout
userStories.description: As a developer, I want to convert one PDF into a predictable output directory so that I can inspect and compile the generated Typst files.
acceptanceCriteria: The CLI accepts an input PDF path and an output directory path.
On success, the output directory contains one main .typ file and any referenced asset files.
The help text documents required arguments and key options, including --strict.
Success, success with warnings, and fatal failure behaviors are documented and covered by tests.
cargo fmt --check passes
cargo test passes

userStories.title: Emit Typst from digital PDFs
userStories.description: As a developer, I want text-based PDFs to be converted into valid Typst structure so that I can reuse existing digital documents without manual rewriting.
acceptanceCriteria: Digital PDF text is extracted in reading order.
Detectable structure, including headings, paragraphs, lists, and code-like blocks, is mapped to valid Typst syntax.
Document order is preserved across multi-page inputs.
Unsupported structures are surfaced through diagnostics instead of being silently dropped.
cargo fmt --check passes
cargo test passes

userStories.title: Add OCR support for scanned Korean and English PDFs
userStories.description: As a developer, I want scanned PDFs to go through OCR so that Korean and English mixed documents can also be converted into Typst.
acceptanceCriteria: Image-based scanned PDFs can be processed through an OCR path.
The default OCR configuration targets mixed Korean and English documents.
OCR output flows into the same downstream Typst generation pipeline used for digital PDFs.
Unavailable OCR support or low-confidence OCR results produce diagnostics that explain the cause and impact.
cargo fmt --check passes
cargo test passes

userStories.title: Convert tables, images, and captions
userStories.description: As a developer, I want non-text elements preserved in Typst so that converted documents remain useful beyond plain text extraction.
acceptanceCriteria: Detected images are extracted as reusable asset files and referenced from generated Typst.
Tables with sufficient structural confidence are emitted as Typst table elements.
Image and table captions are preserved when they can be detected.
When rich element conversion is incomplete or impossible, the degraded elements are recorded.
cargo fmt --check passes
cargo test passes

userStories.title: Support strict and non-strict conversion modes
userStories.description: As a developer, I want to choose between best-effort conversion and fail-fast validation so that I can use the CLI for both exploration and strict pipelines.
acceptanceCriteria: The default mode continues conversion as far as possible and records warnings when unsupported elements are encountered.
The --strict mode treats unsupported or incomplete conversion as a failure instead of a warning.
Diagnostics include page-level or element-level context when available.
Tests verify the difference between default mode and --strict mode for the same failure case.
cargo fmt --check passes
cargo test passes

userStories.title: Validate compileability and regression coverage
userStories.description: As a developer, I want automated regression checks on sample PDFs so that converted Typst output stays compilable as the converter evolves.
acceptanceCriteria: Sample PDFs under data/ can be exercised by automated tests.
cargo test verifies that generated .typ output for supported samples is actually compilable.
Regression coverage includes at least one digital PDF and one scanned mixed Korean and English PDF.
Test failures identify the affected sample and the conversion stage that regressed.
cargo fmt --check passes
cargo test passes
